title: Matter Profitability Agent
shortDescription: Computes the true profit per matter and per matter type, accounting for billable rates, write-downs, costs, and partner time.

workflow.label: Pull time + invoices
workflow.detail: Entries, billed, paid, and written off

workflow.label: Compute realization
workflow.detail: Per matter, partner, and practice area

seo.title: AI Matter Profitability Agent for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Compute true profit per matter and per matter type. Billable rates, write-downs, costs, and partner time included. The intake decision that gets sharper every quarter.

hero.headline: AI Matter Profitability Agent
hero.subheadline: Quarterly view of profit per matter and per matter type, with the realization rate, the partner-time load, and the cost recovery folded in. The intake conversations get sharper every quarter as the picture becomes evidence-based.
bullets: True profit per matter, not just gross fees
Realization rate per matter type and per partner
Partner and associate time-cost folded into the picture
Quarterly trends so seasonal patterns surface
hero.guarantee: Blueprint refunded if we don't leave you with a clear path forward.

scenario.heading: The matter type you would have kept taking
paragraphs: Your firm has been taking small-dollar uncontested probate matters as a courtesy for years. The Matter Profitability agent runs the quarterly numbers: 18 of those matters last quarter, average gross fee of $1,800, average realization of 71% after write-downs, average partner time of 4.2 hours. The matters are losing money once you account for the partner load. The conversation at the next partners' meeting moves from intuition to evidence: raise the minimum fee, push these to a paralegal-led process, or stop taking them.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Matter Profitability agent does
bullets: Computes profit per matter using billable rates, write-downs, costs, and time entries
Rolls profit up by matter type, practice area, and originating partner
Tracks realization rate per matter type and per associate
Surfaces matter types where profitability has shifted quarter over quarter
Posts the analysis as a quarterly partners' digest

integrations: Practice management system for matter records and time entries
Accounting system for fee revenue, write-downs, and cost ledger
Slack or email for the digest delivery

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: The agent is read-only on every system it touches. The analysis is internal-only and scoped to the partners. Per-attorney profitability views are particularly sensitive; the access controls are explicit and tunable per firm.

faq.question: What about contingency matters where the fee isn't realized for years?
faq.answer: Contingency profitability is reported as a forecast based on the matter's expected value and the cost-to-date, then trued up when the matter resolves. The forecast and the actual are tracked separately so the firm can see whether the early valuations are landing close to reality.

faq.question: How does it handle pro bono and reduced-fee work?
faq.answer: Pro bono and reduced-fee matters are tracked separately so they don't distort the practice-area realization picture. The firm can still see the cost (partner and associate time) of the pro bono book to support pro bono reporting.
